Plastic (PET).notes: Einwegpfand: €0.25 for plastic bottles bearing the Pfand logo. Return at supermarket.

prep: Place in Gelbe Tonne / Gelber Sack
Plastic.notes: The Duales System accepts plastic, metal, and composite packaging marked with the Grüner Punkt.

Glass.notes: Do not use glass containers at night (Nachtruhe). Ceramics and heat-resistant glass NOT accepted.

Aluminum.notes: Pfand applies to aluminum cans with the Pfand logo (€0.25).

Cardboard.notes: Blaue Tonne for paper and cardboard. Pizza boxes with heavy grease → Restmüll.

Paper.notes: Books, catalogues, envelopes (without plastic windows) all go in the blue bin.

prep: Use only compostable or paper bags
Organic.notes: Braune Tonne. Meat, fish, and cooked food: regulations vary by municipality (Gemeinde).

prep: Take to Wertstoffhof or retailer take-back point
Do NOT place in household bins
E-Waste.notes: ElektroG law requires free take-back from retailers with ≥400 m² floor space.

Mixed Waste.notes: Reduce Restmüll by separating glass, paper, and packaging properly.

prep: Use a textile collection container or donate
Textile.notes: Altkleider containers are widespread in Germany (DRK, Humana, etc.).
